rationale: The citing paper gives Zhang and Wedel (2009) a specific empirical role in the literature review, summarizing its core finding that promotions for previously purchased goods are more effective online than offline. It then uses that result to position its own contribution in a different consumption context (digital content with more variety-seeking) and to motivate why spillover and substitution patterns may differ. This is more than a passing list citation because the cited study is used to frame expected differences and interpret the paper's positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Zhang and Wedel (2009) as a prior benchmark on targeted promotion effectiveness across online versus offline channels, then contrasts that CPG-focused setting with its own e-book context to motivate new analysis of cross-genre spillovers and opportunity costs.
